draft.introduction: The crucifixion in Mark is stark: darkness covers the land, Jesus cries out in Aramaic “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?” and dies with a loud cry. The temple veil tears in two.

translation.text: And when the sixth hour had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour. And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?” And Jesus uttered a loud cry and breathed his last. And the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom. And when the centurion saw that in this way he breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God.”
translation.original_text: καὶ γενομένης ὥρας ἕκτης σκότος ἐγένετο ἐφ᾽ ὅλην τὴν γῆν

translator_notes.note: Jesus’ cry quotes Psalm 22:1. Debate continues over whether this is genuine despair or liturgical invocation of a psalm that ends in triumph. Mark offers no resolution. The centurion’s confession comes not after a miracle but after watching a man die.
